definitions.technical: Assets pledged as security for a financial obligation. In decentralized finance, stablecoins frequently serve as collateral for loans and trading positions, meaning that a stablecoin depeg can trigger forced liquidations across many protocols simultaneously.
definitions.lay: Assets put up as a guarantee when borrowing money. In crypto lending, stablecoins are commonly used as collateral. When a stablecoin loses its dollar peg, the collateral loses value, which can trigger a wave of forced selling across many platforms.
definitions.parenthetical: (assets pledged as security for financial obligations, including stablecoins used to back DeFi loans and positions)
definitions.legal_significance: The GENIUS Act restricts how permitted stablecoin issuers may use reserve assets, prohibiting rehypothecation and commingling. Stablecoins from non-permitted issuers cannot be used as margin or collateral by futures commission merchants.
